words.definition: Can be eaten; fit for consumption as food.
words.example: The mushroom was safe and edible.
The bread had gone hard and inedible.
The camp had a tap of potable water.
The stream water was surprisingly drinkable.
The coffee was cold and undrinkable.
Soup is more digestible than steak.
Raw beans can be indigestible.
Her whisper was barely audible.
The dog whistle is inaudible to humans.
The scar was still faintly visible.
Ultraviolet light is invisible to the eye.
Please write in legible handwriting.

words.definition: Not clear enough to be read.
words.example: The doctor's signature was illegible.
The novel is short and highly readable.

words.definition: Able to be touched or clearly perceived.
words.example: We need tangible proof, not rumours.
Goodwill is an intangible asset.

words.definition: So intense as to be almost felt physically.
words.example: The tension in the room was palpable.

words.definition: Unable to be felt by touch.
words.example: A fine, impalpable dust covered the shelves.

words.definition: Able to be easily carried or moved.
words.example: She bought a portable charger for the trip.
Easter is a movable feast.
The safe was an immovable steel block.

words.definition: Able to be taken off or away.
words.example: The cover has a removable lining.

words.definition: Able to be washed without damage.
words.example: Choose washable paints for children.
Wrap the breakable items in bubble wrap.
The phone case is nearly unbreakable.
Yoga makes the spine more flexible.

words.definition: Able to be hammered or pressed into shape.
words.example: Gold is the most malleable metal.

words.definition: Able to be drawn out into a thin wire.
words.example: Copper is ductile and conducts well.

words.definition: Able to be melted by heat.
words.example: Solder is a fusible alloy.
Sugar is soluble in water.
Sand is insoluble in water.

words.definition: Able to be mixed to form a homogeneous liquid.
words.example: Ethanol and water are miscible.
Oil and water are immiscible.
Petrol is highly flammable.
The label warned the solvent was inflammable.

words.definition: Able to catch fire and burn.
words.example: Store combustible waste away from heat.

words.definition: Unable to catch fire and burn.
words.example: The wall is lined with non-combustible board.
Twelve is divisible by three.

words.definition: Unable to be divided or separated.
words.example: The nation stands as an indivisible whole.
'Chair' is a countable noun.
'Water' is an uncountable noun.
She has innumerable reasons to refuse.
There has been measurable progress.
His kindness brought immeasurable comfort.
Use an erasable pen on the whiteboard.

words.definition: Unable to be removed or erased.
words.example: The event left an indelible memory.
Bring your own reusable bag.
The bottle is fully recyclable.
She carries a refillable water bottle.

words.definition: Able to be decomposed by living organisms.
words.example: The cutlery is made of biodegradable plastic.

words.definition: Likely to decay or spoil quickly.
words.example: Keep perishable food in the fridge.

words.definition: Enduring forever; not liable to decay.
words.example: Diamonds are considered nearly imperishable.
Most childhood infections are curable.
He suffers from an incurable condition.
The disease is treatable if caught early.
Many accidents at work are preventable.
The delay was entirely avoidable.
A short delay is unavoidable.
Conflict seemed inevitable.

words.definition: Unable to be avoided or escaped.
words.example: The conclusion is inescapable.
His anger is easily explicable.
The strange lights remain inexplicable.

words.definition: Too great to be expressed in words.
words.example: She felt an ineffable joy at the summit.

words.definition: Too intense to be described in words.
words.example: He gazed at her with unutterable love.
The view was indescribable.

words.definition: Too taboo to be spoken of.
words.example: Certain topics were unmentionable at dinner.

words.definition: Too bad or shocking to be expressed.
words.example: They suffered unspeakable cruelty.

words.definition: Impossible to travel along or over.
words.example: Snow made the road impassable.

words.definition: Impossible to pass or see through.
words.example: The jungle was impenetrable.

words.definition: Unable to be captured or broken into.
words.example: The fortress was thought impregnable.
The village is inaccessible by car.

words.definition: Not able to be attained or reached.
words.example: Perfection is unattainable.
The obstacles proved insurmountable.
The champion seemed invincible.

words.definition: Unable to occur or be done.
words.example: It's impossible to be in two places at once.

words.definition: Possible to do easily or conveniently.
words.example: The plan is technically feasible.

words.definition: Able to be brought about; attainable.
words.example: Set small, achievable goals.

words.definition: Able to be reached or accomplished.
words.example: A distinction is attainable with effort.
The deadline is tight but doable.
Break the task into manageable steps.
Every problem here is solvable.
Whether he acted wisely is debatable.

words.definition: Able to be argued or maintained.
words.example: It is arguable that she deserves the award.
His involvement is barely deniable.

words.definition: Unable to be denied or disputed.
words.example: There is undeniable evidence of fraud.
The claim is easily refutable.
The DNA match is irrefutable.

words.definition: Able to be checked or proved true.
words.example: Give only verifiable facts in your essay.
She gave a credible account of events.
He told an incredible tale of survival.
She's a reliable friend.
Buy a dependable used car.
The ending of the film was predictable.
The crash was due to unforeseeable weather.

words.definition: Able to be likened to another.
words.example: The two houses are comparable in size.

words.definition: So good it has no equal.
words.example: She sang with incomparable grace.
The salary is negotiable.

words.definition: Not open to discussion or modification.
words.example: Safety rules are non-negotiable.
The jacket is reversible.
The damage to the reef is irreversible.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Can be eaten; fit for consumption as food."?
quiz.explanation: Edible (adjective) — Can be eaten; fit for consumption as food.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Not fit to be eaten."?
quiz.explanation: Inedible (adjective) — Not fit to be eaten.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Safe to drink."?
quiz.explanation: Potable (adjective) — Safe to drink.

quiz.explanation: Drinkable (adjective) — Suitable or safe to drink.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Not fit to be drunk."?
quiz.explanation: Undrinkable (adjective) — Not fit to be drunk.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Difficult or impossible to digest."?
quiz.explanation: Indigestible (adjective) — Difficult or impossible to digest.

quiz.explanation: Audible (adjective) — Loud enough to be heard.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Unable to be heard."?
quiz.explanation: Inaudible (adjective) — Unable to be heard.
